layers.name: Framework Runtime
layers.description: Core evolutionary framework implementation, algorithms, backends, and package-level runtime modules under evolve/.

layers.name: Quality Assurance
layers.description: Automated verification assets including unit, integration, end-to-end, and benchmark tests under tests/.

layers.name: Experiments and Examples
layers.description: Runnable demonstration scripts and experiment entrypoints showing framework usage patterns under examples/.

layers.name: Documentation and Learning
layers.description: User and developer-facing documentation, tutorials, generated docs assets, and top-level contributor guides.

layers.name: Specifications and Planning
layers.description: Spec-driven development artifacts, plans, contracts, checklists, and specification tooling scaffolding.

layers.name: Automation and Governance
layers.description: Repository governance assets, agent/prompt definitions, CI workflows, and hook automation orchestration.

layers.name: Project Configuration and Infrastructure
layers.description: Build, packaging, environment, editor, container, and repository-level operational configuration files.

tour.title: Orient Around Project Intent
tour.description: Start with the repository-level narrative and packaging contract. This establishes what evolve-framework promises (declarative evolutionary experimentation) and how users consume it before diving into internals.

tour.title: Learn The UnifiedConfig-First Model
tour.description: Read the declarative configuration layer that the README centers on. UnifiedConfig and tracking config define most user-facing decisions, while the advanced guide clarifies how the schema scales to complex workflows.
tour.languageLesson: In this codebase, Python dataclass-style config objects are treated as durable experiment contracts, not just runtime convenience wrappers.

tour.title: Trace How Config Becomes A Running Engine
tour.description: Follow the core execution path from declarative config into factory assembly, registry resolution, and engine runtime. This is the central control flow for nearly every experiment in the framework.

tour.title: Inspect Representation, Evaluation, And Selection Pressure
tour.description: Study how genomes are represented and scored, then how diversity and multi-objective support shape search pressure. These modules explain what is being evolved and how fitness trade-offs are handled.

tour.title: Dive Into ERP And Sexual Selection
tour.description: Move into the framework's distinguishing ERP capability. The reproduction engine plus ERP-focused examples and best-practices guide show how mating intent, matchability, and protocol evolution are modeled in practice.

tour.title: Understand Observability And Experiment Tracking
tour.description: Connect declarative tracking settings to runtime tracker behavior and a concrete end-to-end demo. This step mirrors the README's emphasis on reproducibility and experiment-level observability.

tour.title: Map Execution Backends And Performance Paths
tour.description: Review the execution backends to see how the same evolution logic runs sequentially, in parallel, or with accelerated evaluators. This explains where optional performance dependencies plug in.
tour.languageLesson: Backend abstraction here is Python-protocol driven: algorithms stay stable while execution strategies swap underneath them.

tour.title: Walk The Learning Curriculum
tour.description: Use the tutorials as a progressive curriculum from UnifiedConfig basics to ERP workflows. The notebook sequence reflects the same conceptual layering described in the README.

tour.title: Verify Guarantees Through The Test Pyramid
tour.description: Confirm behavior at multiple confidence levels: strict config unit tests, declarative engine integration tests, and executable tutorial validation. This mirrors the framework's reproducibility and correctness goals.

tour.title: Read The Spec-Driven Roadmap And Governance
tour.description: Finish by mapping implemented architecture back to living specs, contribution guidance, and CI enforcement. This reveals how new research features are planned and integrated without breaking the core framework contract.
